lines: Patient is a 65 year old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
He presents with a 2 day history of fever, chills, and cough.
Vital signs: temperature 38.5 C, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg, heart rate 110 bpm.
Physical exam reveals lung crackles and wheezing.
Lab results: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, platelets 80 10^9/L.
Chest X-ray shows bilateral infiltrates.
Patient is being treated with antibiotics and fluids.
Differential diagnosis includes pneumonia and sepsis.
Plan is to continue antibiotics and monitor labs and vitals closely.
Patient is being considered for ICU admission.
Will re-evaluate in 24 hours.
Labs and vitals will be monitored closely.
Patient's condition is being closely monitored.
Will consider vasopressors if blood pressure does not improve.
Patient's family has been informed of the situation.
Patient is being treated with oxygen therapy.
Patient's oxygen saturation is being monitored closely.
Patient's condition has not improved.
